description: Knowledge base for improving handwritten Greek medical note transcription. Updated with corrections from doctor review.

formatting_rules.medical_terms: PURPLE (#7B2D8E) + UPPERCASE — all disease names, conditions, medical terms
formatting_rules.uncertain_text: RED (#CC0000) + [text?] — words not recognized or not in dictionary

confirmed.ΟΛΛ: Οξεία Λεμφοβλαστική Λευχαιμία (ALL - Acute Lymphoblastic Leukemia)
confirmed.Φ: Φυσιολογικό or negative (when used for allergies = No)
confirmed.ΤΚΕ: Ταχύτητα Καθίζησης Ερυθρών (ESR - Erythrocyte Sedimentation Rate)
confirmed.ΠΔΚ: needs confirmation — seen in clinical exam after Οιδήματα

unconfirmed_ask_doctor.Χ/Γ: Seen after surgical procedures — possibly Χειρουργείο/Γενικό? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.ΑΙ: Seen after Ακουστικό νευρίνωμα — possibly αριστερό (left)? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.SE: Seen in cardiac exam — possibly systolic ejection? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.6λφ: Unknown — seen next to Μητέρα in family history. ASK DOCTOR
Seen in instructions — possibly Κλινικός Ομιλιτικός? ASK DOCTOR
unconfirmed_ask_doctor.ΠΔΚ: Seen after Οιδήματα in clinical exam. ASK DOCTOR

note_structure.description: The doctor follows a consistent template for each patient visit
sections_visit: Patient demographics (name, DOB, address, phone, AMKA)
Family history: Self | Father | Mother (3-column). Mother section includes ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ age.
Lab results (empty placeholder if none for this visit)

confirmed.ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ_looks_like_ΕΝΗΜΕΡΩΤΗΣ: In handwriting, ΕΜΜΗΝΟΠΑΥΣΗ can be misread as ΕΝΗΜΕΡΩΤΗΣ — look for it in family history context followed by age
confirmed.Υπεραλδοστερονισμία_was_ΥΠΕΡΛΙΠΙΔΑΙΜΙΑ: Misread ΥΠΕΡΛΙΠΙΔΑΙΜΙΑ as Υπεραλδοστερονισμία — similar letter patterns
confirmed.Προαλβουμινημία_was_ΠΡΟΔΙΑΒΗΤΗΣ: Misread ΠΡΟΔΙΑΒΗΤΗΣ as Προαλβουμινημία — ΠΡΟ prefix correct but rest differs
confirmed.2019_was_2012: Year misread — 2 can look like 9 in this handwriting. Flag uncertain years in red.

suspected.description: Patterns we think we see but need doctor confirmation
items: Doctor writes circled numbers ①②③ for enumerated items
κφ is used as shorthand across all exam systems to mean normal
Lab reference ranges written as (ΦΤ: X-Y) or (ΦΤ < X)
Arrow → used to indicate 'leads to' or 'result is'

hallucination_warnings: Patient004: Θυρεοειδής (ΤΑΚ) and Μασχάλες (κφ) were NOT in the original scan but were generated. Be careful not to add exam items that aren't present.
Always cross-reference every exam line against the scan. Do not assume standard exam items are present.

corrections_log.description: Corrections from doctor review. Each entry maps to a row number in the digitized document.

Patient004_1_v2.note: Phone numbers should always be formatted as XXXX XXX XXX

Referral was from a friend, not a psychiatrist. The original handwriting was φίλη, not Ψυχ. Note: Ψυχ. IS a valid abbreviation for Ψυχίατρος (Psychiatrist) — the error was in reading the handwriting, not the abbreviation meaning.
Father's condition was hyperlipidemia, not hyperaldosteronism.
Father's condition was prediabetes. ΠΡΟ prefix was correct.
Χρ. = Χρόνια (Chronic). Format: abbreviation + full condition name.
In original, this was written to the left of the mother section.

Patient004_1_v2.corrected: Add ΟΙΔΗΜΑΤΑ ΠΔΚ between Νευρολογικά and next item
Patient004_1_v2.note: Edema finding was present in original but missed in transcription.
This should be Αγγειακά (Vascular). Θυρεοειδής was not in the original.
This exam item was hallucinated. Not present in original scan.

common_medical_terms.description: Greek medical terms frequently seen in this doctor's notes. Display in PURPLE + UPPERCASE.

medication_names.description: Medications/pharmaceuticals seen in notes. Display in PURPLE + UPPERCASE. Maintain as running database.

Patient004_1_v7.output_format: v7 hybrid — transcription + original JPG appended as full page

uncertain_rows: 33 (Ca Προστάτου under mother — seems wrong for female)
Multiple ultrasound rows (48-60) — very dense handwriting, many uncertain readings
74-83 (Visit 2 instructions — multiple uncertain drug names and dosages)

document_format_v7.description: Hybrid format: structured digital transcription followed by original scan appended at end
document_format_v7.approach: Digitize metadata accurately (patient info, dates, meds, labs), accept imperfect clinical note transcription, append original for reference
document_format_v7.source_format: JPG preferred over PNG (7x smaller for scans, no visible quality loss)
document_format_v7.image_embedding: Original scan goes in a SEPARATE docx section with minimal margins (0.5cm), no headers/footers, image scaled to fill page using transformation width matching page width at 96 DPI
document_format_v7.image_sizing_lesson: docx-js ImageRun transformation uses pixels at 96 DPI. A4 with 0.5cm margins = 793px wide x 1138px tall. Must use separate section for different margins — cannot mix margin sizes within one section.
document_format_v7.fit_to_page_rule: Always fit-to-page: calculate scaleW = 793/imgW, scaleH = 1138/imgH, use Math.min(scaleW, scaleH). This ensures the ENTIRE image fits regardless of aspect ratio. Wide images are width-constrained, tall images are height-constrained.
document_format_v7.jpg_quality: 75 at 150 DPI is sweet spot for scan embedding (~1.5MB/page). For small source images already at screen res, just embed as-is.

differences_from_004: No AMKA recorded for this patient
Family history includes siblings (not just parents)
Multiple diagnostic tests between visits (TRIPLEX, ECHO, DEXA)
Much denser note — 2 pages vs Patient004's single page
Follow-up visit includes additional medication (ΠΡΟΣΘΕΤΗ ΑΓΩΓΗ)
